name: Assurance Runtime
summary: Turns repository and process state into bounded, content-addressed observations without making acceptance decisions.

invariants: Observations never decide whether a Change is acceptable.
Command Observation v2 keeps support, control trigger, confirmed close, actual termination, and two raw-byte streams orthogonal; captured text is never the authority.
The local-direct-node-posix-v1 profile supports direct argv only on Linux or macOS under Node major 22, 24, or 25; every other profile or host is reported unsupported before spawn.
Timeout and cancellation request bounded process-group termination, retain prior stream bytes, and do not return a confirmed observation until close; an unconfirmed close cannot become successful.
Injected runner reports are strictly normalized and bounded; permissive ok flags, contradictory exit and signal facts, and unknown result shapes cannot synthesize success.
Failed or truncated Git observations fail closed.

One repository observation emits a bounded complete canonical tracked-path set and exact digest, or reports the binding unavailable on command failure, truncation, malformed paths, duplicates, or resource-limit exhaustion.

Repository Source observation v1 accepts only an ordered bounded list of canonical exact concrete paths whose every member belongs to the accepted round's exact tracked-path facts; untracked paths, selector expansion, and disclosure policy remain outside this product and Context Kernel owns scope compilation.

Each source round privately resolves and validates the real repository root and identity, exact Git binding and tracked-path facts, requested path set, and regular-file identity before and after reading; its reproducible manifest and composite observation bind only repository identity, Git and inventory digests, ordered paths, byte lengths, and byte digests, and only two consecutive identical composite digests within a bounded three-round window may issue a product.

A Repository Source Product is an opaque process-local WeakMap-branded capability; a plain record, proxy, clone, serialized value, or product from another source binding cannot project a manifest or disclose bytes.

The public source projection requires a complete exact expected repository path, Git binding digest, tracked-path facts digest, and ordered path set; Assurance Runtime re-observes repository identity, rejects every missing or partial expectation, and emits only repository-identity, Git, inventory, path-set, manifest, and product digests plus ordered path, byte-length, and content-digest entries, never an absolute root or source body.

Byte reads return defensive copies captured by the stable product and never re-read the filesystem; unknown paths, mutation aliasing, missing or extra entries, drift, traversal, symlink, non-regular or unreadable files, duplicates, malformed input, and resource exhaustion fail closed.

Repository Source Products establish exact bounded local source facts only; they do not authorize scope, enforce filesystem access, infer semantic impact, settle Worker execution, create Evidence or Knowledge Closure, or decide acceptance.
